channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Developments in Iran conflict: Casualties in Tehran and US-Iran statements
item.description: United States President Donald Trump asserted on Tuesday that his country has won the conflict in Iran, dismissing reports suggesting otherwise as 'fake news'. US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth claimed...

item.title: Essendon’s leadership struggles to admit the need for a rebuild despite clear signs on the field
item.description: The article examines the growing perception that the Essendon Football Club is in the midst of a rebuild, even though its leadership refuses to label it as such. Since Andrew Welsh became club preside...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens basketball with Kawe arena plans and Dar City BAL preparations
item.description: Tanzania's basketball scene is getting big support as Minister Paul Makonda confirmed government plans to build a modern multi-purpose basketball arena in Kawe. This project is part of wider efforts t...

item.title: Trump's $200 Billion Request Signals Major Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: The Trump administration has requested an additional $200 billion for its military operations against Iran, a move that underscores the scale of the U.S.'s growing involvement in the Middle East. This...

item.title: Delhi Court Sentences Kashmiri Separatist Asiya Andrabi to Life Imprisonment, Associates to 30 Years
item.description: In a significant ruling, a Delhi court on Tuesday awarded life imprisonment to Kashmiri separatist and Dukhtaran-e-Millat chief, Asiya Andrabi, for conspiring against the Indian State. The court also ...

item.title: 33 Bodies Found in Mass Grave in Kericho, Including 25 Children
item.description: A mass grave containing 33 bodies has been discovered in Kericho, with 25 children and 8 adults among the victims, as confirmed by Dr. Richard Njoroge, a pathologist leading the investigation. The exh...

item.title: SCA Provides Guidance on Handling Cross-Border Insolvency Surpluses
item.description: The South African Supreme Court of Appeal (SCA) has delivered a landmark judgment in the case of Scheer v Wagner N.O. & Others, clarifying how cross-border insolvency matters are treated in South Afri...

item.title: Proteas Women fall to White Ferns as Amelia Kerr dominates in Christchurch
item.description: The Proteas Women suffered another tough outing on their New Zealand tour, falling to a 92-run defeat against the White Ferns at Hagley Oval on Wednesday. New Zealand captain Amelia Kerr was unstoppab...

item.title: New Excavations Reveal Mohenjo Daro’s Urban Origins from 3300 BC
item.description: Recent excavations at Mohenjo Daro have uncovered compelling evidence that traces the city's urban roots to the Kot Diji Phase, around 3300-2600 BC. Archaeologists working on the project, led by exper...

item.title: Public satisfaction with the NHS improves modestly amid ongoing frustration over long waits
item.description: Public satisfaction with the NHS has increased for the first time since 2019, rising from a record low of 21% last year to 26%, according to the latest British Social Attitudes survey. Dissatisfaction...

item.title: Understanding Why Tuberculosis Still Kills Many Nigerians Despite Free Treatment
item.description: Even with medical progress and free TB treatment available across Nigeria, plenty people still dey die from the disease every year. The main wahala na late diagnosis, lack of proper awareness, and the...

item.title: Pakistan-Iran Border Trade Persists Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: Despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, border trade between Pakistan and Iran has continued without major interruption. Key border crossings including Taftan, Gabad, Mand, and Panjgur remain open...

item.title: Explosion and Fire at Valero Refinery in Texas After Fluid Release
item.description: On March 23, 2026, a sudden fluid release at Valero's Port Arthur refinery triggered an explosion and fire, leading to a temporary shutdown of the 380,000-barrel-per-day oil refinery. Fortunately, the...

item.title: SC Rules on Preliminary Hearing in Contempt of Court Cases
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has reinforced the importance of a preliminary hearing in contempt of court matters. In a ruling delivered on March 25, 2026, the court emphasized that before framing cha...

item.title: Tanzania and South Africa Strengthen Collaboration to Promote Kiswahili in Schools
item.description: Tanzania and South Africa are increasing efforts to implement the 2022 Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) that focuses on boosting Kiswahili teaching in South African schools. This agreement was made t...

item.title: Iran conflict pushes up costs of medical equipment and supplies in India
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran has caused a notable surge in the cost of medical supplies in India. Manufacturers of surgical gowns, protective gear, syringes, dialysis kits, and dental implants ...

item.title: US Sends Peace Proposal as Iran Allows Safe Passage for Oil Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has proposed a peace plan to Iran as Tehran announced it will allow 'non-hostile' oil vessels through the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The move comes after weeks of heightened...

item.title: Senate inquiry highlights rise of climate misinformation and its impact on Australian democracy
item.description: Australia is experiencing a surge of misinformation and disinformation concerning climate change and energy, intensified by the use of artificial intelligence and the widespread reach of social media,...

item.title: Using cost-per-wear to encourage sustainable clothing choices
item.description: A recent study explores how informing consumers about the 'cost per wear' of clothing could influence purchasing behaviour and promote more sustainable fashion. Cost per wear is calculated by dividing...

item.title: Holy Week Travel: Ferry and Bus Fares Frozen, Says Marcos
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that ferry and bus operators have committed not to increase fares during the upcoming Holy Week, aiming to ease the financial burden on Filipinos, e...
